mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during post-recession recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series in out-of-sample backtests, or 60-day forward returns turn negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate profit margins and force negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive during industrial production contractions, or the mechanism shows no predictive edge outside of late-cycle macro environments.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stagnant federal funds rate indicate eroding real yields and delayed policy tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% monthly while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when tested against a univariate CPI model.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns show no directional bias during periods of consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears that force rapid portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns during mild GDP contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment signals labor market inefficiency that suppresses wage growth and corporate pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn positive when industrial production outpaces unemployment declines, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing during recovery phases.
